description: Service that consumes events from Kafka topic 'events.raw' (produced by the upstream EVENT_PRODUCER service) and writes each event to blob storage at key 'events/<received_at>-<id>.json'. The Kafka payload is JSON with EXACT field names: id (str), received_at (str ISO 8601), headers (dict[str, str]), payload (str). The ConsumedEvent entity carries those four fields verbatim. Two bounded contexts: Consumption (Kafka consume + parse) and Archival (blob write).
acceptance_criteria: Given a Kafka message containing a serialized event with keys id, received_at, headers, payload, When consume_event is called, Then result is a ConsumedEvent
Given a malformed Kafka message, When consume_event is called, Then an error is raised
Given a ConsumedEvent and a blob storage adapter, When archive_event is called, Then the event is written to key 'events/<received_at>-<id>.json'
Given a blob write failure, When archive_event is called, Then an error is raised
Given the same ConsumedEvent twice, When archive_event is called, Then the second write is idempotent (overwrite)
